title: Mating in Captivity: Unlocking Erotic Intelligence
subtitle: A provocative exploration of the tension between domestic security and erotic desire in long-term relationships.
author: Esther Perel
themes: The paradox of domesticity and desire — security kills excitement
Erotic intelligence as a learnable skill, not a biological given
The role of distance, mystery, and separateness in sustaining desire
Why good people have affairs — and what affairs reveal about relationships
excerpt: Why does desire fade in long-term relationships? Esther Perel, a couples therapist with a provocative perspective, argues that the very qualities we cultivate in committed relationships — security, predictability, mutual care — can suffocate erotic desire. The solution is not to abandon commitment but to cultivate 'erotic intelligence': the ability to hold the tension between love and desire, intimacy and otherness, domesticity and adventure.
